raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis, contribution framing, and research gap articulated in the draft.","R1.2":"A general reaction to the artifact would centrally include whether the theoretical argument chain is coherent.","R1.3":"The draft makes empirical and theoretical claims, so checking whether reported results support those claims is expected.","R1.4":"The artifact contains explicit novelty claims about sketched Krylov methods, making novelty assessment central.","R1.5":"A broad opinion on the draft can reasonably include the ordering and narrative arc of the presented sections.","R1.6":"The request naturally includes sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision in the research prose.","R1.7":"Because the passage relies on named prior works and theorem attribution, citation and scholarship responsibilities are central.","RX.1":"Any response should disclose uncertainty because the artifact is only an excerpt and external verification is not provided.","RX.2":"The projection must preserve responsibility for avoiding inflated conclusions about novelty, guarantees, and empirical strength.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within the requested evaluation scope and not execute edits or perform the delegation.","RX.4":"The response should avoid advice or assessments that could mislead downstream research claims or publication decisions.","RX.5":"The response should preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact rather than introducing unsupported sources or assumptions."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis, contribution framing, and research gap articulated in the draft.
rationale.R1.2: A general reaction to the artifact would centrally include whether the theoretical argument chain is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes empirical and theoretical claims, so checking whether reported results support those claims is expected.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact contains explicit novelty claims about sketched Krylov methods, making novelty assessment central.
rationale.R1.5: A broad opinion on the draft can reasonably include the ordering and narrative arc of the presented sections.
rationale.R1.6: The request naturally includes sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision in the research prose.
rationale.R1.7: Because the passage relies on named prior works and theorem attribution, citation and scholarship responsibilities are central.
rationale.RX.1: Any response should disclose uncertainty because the artifact is only an excerpt and external verification is not provided.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must preserve responsibility for avoiding inflated conclusions about novelty, guarantees, and empirical strength.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within the requested evaluation scope and not execute edits or perform the delegation.
rationale.RX.4: The response should avoid advice or assessments that could mislead downstream research claims or publication decisions.
rationale.RX.5: The response should preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact rather than introducing unsupported sources or assumptions.
